symptoms.symptom_name: Acute autonomic stress symptoms — palpitations, breathlessness, tremor, tearfulness with abrupt onset on a specific trigger

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Trigger-locked abrupt autonomic arousal that settles with reassurance is characteristic of a situational panic response.
Palpitations are non-specific; a cardiac red-flag screen is required before attribution.
Absence of bleeding is a pertinent negative reducing suspicion of symptomatic colorectal disease; screening (not workup) remains appropriate.

symptoms.symptom_name: No change in bowel habit and no unexplained weight loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Asymptomatic status keeps the patient in the screening (not diagnostic) pathway.

symptoms.symptom_name: No crushing/central/radiating chest pain; chest sensation eases with calming

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of ischaemic-character chest pain and resolution with calming argue against ACS; the AI still cannot obtain an ECG remotely and retains a chest-pain safety-net.

symptoms.symptom_name: First-degree relative (sibling) recently died of colorectal cancer

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A first-degree relative with colorectal cancer modifies risk category and reinforces the value of screening completion and GP risk-stratification.
Recent bereavement from the same disease is the proximate psychological trigger for the panic response.

Sympathetic-arousal physiology consistent with an acute stress response once cardiac red flags are excluded; sealed clinician measurement — the AI Doctor infers arousal from the patient's own report, not a measured vital.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unremarkable cardiovascular, respiratory and abdominal examination (soft non-tender abdomen, clear chest, normal heart sounds, no masses)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No palpable mass or hepatomegaly; benign abdominal exam — but a normal exam does not exclude occult neoplasia, which is what screening addresses.
Normal cardiorespiratory exam is reassuring but not definitive; requires in-person assessment/ECG the AI cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: iFOBT (immunochemical faecal occult blood test) result

investigation_details.expected_result: Pending — the home screening test has not yet been completed or processed
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Non-completion of screening means occult early colorectal neoplasia could go undetected; a positive result would necessitate colonoscopy.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The iFOBT is the population screening test; completion is the key preventive action for this asymptomatic patient.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Complete and mail the National Bowel Cancer Screening iFOBT kit
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Preventive screening for an asymptomatic eligible adult; supported by graded exposure and GP/practice-nurse assistance to overcome avoidance.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: GP review to clarify risk category given a first-degree relative with colorectal cancer
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A first-degree relative may shift the patient from average to increased risk, potentially altering the recommended screening modality/interval.
